---
title: "parameterised_norm"
generated: true
description: "Per-row Mahalanobis-style normalisation."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.linalg.kernel`](../nitrix-linalg-kernel.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:parameterised_norm -->
```python
parameterised_norm(X: Float[Array, '... n d'], *, theta: Optional[Num[Array, '... d']] = None, squared: bool = False) -> Float[Array, '... n d']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:parameterised_norm -->
Per-row Mahalanobis-style normalisation.

Returns $X / \mathrm{norm}$ (or $X / \mathrm{norm}^2$
with `squared=True`), where $\mathrm{norm}$ is the per-row
Mahalanobis norm under `theta`.  For `theta = None` this is L2
normalisation along the last axis.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:parameterised_norm -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `X` | `Float` | `...` × `n` × `d` | — | Sample tensor of shape $(\dots, n, d)$. |
| `theta` | `Optional[Num[Array, '... d']]` | — | `None` | Optional metric tensor. Same shape conventions as the `theta` argument of [`linear_kernel`](../nitrix-linalg-kernel/linear_kernel.qmd). |
| `squared` | `bool` | — | `False` | If `True`, divide by $\mathrm{norm}^2$ instead of $\mathrm{norm}$. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...` × `n` × `d`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:parameterised_norm -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
